flashcards.front: What class does JTextField extend in Java Swing?
flashcards.back: JTextField extends JTextComponent (indirectly through JTextField → AbstractDocument → JTextComponent). It is part of the javax.swing package and provides a single-line text input field.

flashcards.front: What is the default layout manager for JTextField?
flashcards.back: JTextField uses a FlowLayout by default when added to a container, but typically it inherits the layout manager of its parent container (like JPanel which defaults to FlowLayout).

flashcards.front: How do you set placeholder text in JTextField?
flashcards.back: JTextField doesn't have native placeholder support. However, you can use the putClientProperty method with "JTextField.placeholderText" key (Java 7+) or implement a custom UI component using Nimbus look and feel.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between JTextField.getText() and JTextField.getDocument()?
flashcards.back: getText() returns the text content as a String. getDocument() returns the Document interface that manages the text content, allowing access to content handlers, filters, and document listeners.

flashcards.front: What listener is commonly used with JTextField for real-time input handling?
flashcards.back: DocumentListener is commonly used. Unlike ActionListener (triggered on Enter key), DocumentListener fires on every document change (insert, remove, or change) for real-time validation and feedback.

flashcards.front: How do you make JTextField read-only in Java Swing?
flashcards.back: Call setEditable(false). The field becomes non-editable but still selectable and copyable. Alternatively, setEnabled(false) makes it both non-editable and visually disabled.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of JTextField.setColumns(int)?
flashcards.back: setColumns(int) sets the preferred number of columns (character widths) for the text field, affecting its preferred size but not limiting the actual text length.

flashcards.front: What is a DocumentFilter in JTextField?
flashcards.back: DocumentFilter is used to intercept and modify document changes before they are applied. It allows implementing input restrictions like character limits, uppercase conversion, or numeric-only input.

flashcards.front: How does JPasswordField differ from JTextField?
flashcards.back: JPasswordField extends JTextField and echoes a configurable character (default '*') instead of displaying actual characters. It provides getPassword() returning char[] (not String) for security.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the horizontal alignment in JTextField?
flashcards.back: setHorizontalAlignment(int alignment) controls text alignment (LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT). Use SwingConstants.LEFT, CENTER, or RIGHT constants. Default is LEFT.

flashcards.front: How do you add an ActionListener to JTextField?
flashcards.back: Add using addActionListener(ActionListener). The actionPerformed() is triggered when the user presses Enter while the text field has focus.

flashcards.front: What is the MVC architecture in JTextField?
flashcards.back: Model: PlainDocument stores text; View: UI renders text; Controller: Event handling and user interactions. JTextField implements the MVC pattern allowing separate customization of each component.
